```{r US BUFFET DJ, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("buff_dj_ratio"))

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Wilshire 5000*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r US BUFFET WIL5000, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("buff_wilshire_ratio"))

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Corporate Equity*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r US BUFFET COR, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("buff_corpequity_ratio"))

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, is_quarterly = TRUE))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *Comparison*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r US BUFFET ALL, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = c("buff_sp500_ratio", "buff_dj_ratio", "buff_wilshire_ratio"))

# Compute index
data <- compute_rebase(df = data, base = "2015-01-01")

# Plot serie
div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
    produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = FALSE, title = "Buffet Ratios – Indexed Comparison (2015:01:01 = 100)"))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Commodity  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Commodity

```{=html}
<div class="company-hero">
  <div class="co-body">
    <div class="masthead-title">Commodity</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Oil

```{r OIL TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = 
    paste0(
  '1) <b> Brent — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/BZ=F/" target="_blank">BRENT CRUDE</a></b>. ',
  'Brent Crude Oil Futures measures the price of Brent crude oil for future delivery as traded on the Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), serving as the primary international benchmark for crude oil pricing. Each standard contract represents 1,000 barrels of Brent blend crude oil, a light, sweet crude extracted from fields in the North Sea including the Brent, Forties, Oseberg, Ekofisk, and Troll fields, collectively referred to as the BFOET basket, with a sulfur content of approximately 0.37% and an API gravity of around 38 degrees. Brent is the reference price for approximately two-thirds of globally traded crude oil, making it the dominant international pricing benchmark relative to its US counterpart West Texas Intermediate. Futures prices are sensitive to global supply and demand dynamics, OPEC and OPEC+ production decisions, geopolitical developments in major producing regions, refinery throughput, global inventories, and macroeconomic conditions affecting energy consumption. The front-month contract, the nearest active expiration, serves as the primary market reference for spot Brent pricing. Quoted in US dollars per barrel and updated daily with closing prices.',
  '<br><br>',
  '2) <b>WTI — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/CL=F/" target="_blank">WTI CRUDE</a></b>. ',
  'West Texas Intermediate Crude Oil Futures measures the price of WTI crude oil for future delivery as traded on the New York Mercantile Exchange (NYMEX), operated by CME Group, serving as the primary US benchmark for crude oil pricing. Each standard contract represents 1,000 barrels of West Texas Intermediate crude, a light, sweet crude oil characterized by low sulfur content of approximately 0.24% and an API gravity of around 39.6 degrees, making it particularly well-suited for refining into gasoline and other light petroleum products. Delivery is specified at Cushing, Oklahoma, a major pipeline crossroads and storage hub that serves as the physical pricing point for the US domestic crude market. Relative to its international counterpart Brent crude, WTI has historically traded at a slight premium owing to its superior refining characteristics, though the spread between the two benchmarks fluctuates with US production levels, pipeline capacity constraints, and storage conditions at Cushing. Futures prices are sensitive to US and global supply and demand dynamics, OPEC and OPEC+ production decisions, domestic inventory levels reported by the Energy Information Administration, refinery utilization, and broader macroeconomic conditions affecting energy consumption. The front-month contract, the nearest active expiration, serves as the primary market reference for spot WTI pricing. Quoted in US dollars per barrel and updated daily with closing prices.'))
```

#### *Brent*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r BRENT1, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "fut_brent_crude")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))


```

```{=html}
</div>
```

#### *WTI*

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r WTI1, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "fut_wti_crude")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))


```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Advanced Indicators  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Advanced Indicators

```{=html}
<div class="company-hero">
  <div class="co-body">
    <div class="masthead-title">Advanced Indicators</div>
  </div>
</div>
```


### Walmart

```{r WALM TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/WMT/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "Walmart Inc. is the world\'s largest retailer by revenue, operating a global network of discount stores, supercenters, and e-commerce platforms across the United States and international markets including China, India, Canada, and South Africa. Its core US segment encompasses Walmart stores and Walmart.com, offering grocery, general merchandise, health and wellness, and apparel products at everyday low prices, complemented by the Sam\'s Club membership warehouse segment and international operations including a majority stake in Indian e-commerce platform Flipkart. Because Walmart\'s customer base spans all income levels but skews heavily toward price-sensitive middle- and lower-income households, its same-store sales, customer traffic, and management guidance are closely watched as a real-time barometer of US consumer financial health. As a leading indicator, Walmart\'s quarterly results provide early signals of shifts in consumer confidence, discretionary versus non-discretionary spending trade-offs, food price inflation at the shelf level, and private-label substitution trends, dynamics that often foreshadow movements in official personal consumption expenditure data by several weeks. Quoted in US dollars per share and updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r WALM, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "walmart")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### UPS

```{r UPS TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
series_info_box(
  source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/UPS/" target="_blank">link</a>',
  frequency = "Daily",
  adjustment = "Not Adjusted",
  about = "United Parcel Service, Inc. is the world\'s largest package delivery company by revenue, operating an integrated global logistics network spanning over 200 countries and territories through its US Domestic Package, International Package, and Supply Chain Solutions segments. Its core domestic segment handles the pickup, transportation, and delivery of letters, documents, and packages across the United States via ground and air services, while the international segment covers cross-border small package delivery with particular strength in trans-Atlantic and trans-Pacific trade lanes. Because UPS processes tens of millions of shipments daily on behalf of businesses and consumers across virtually every sector of the economy, including retail, manufacturing, healthcare, technology, and financial services, its volume trends, revenue per piece, and management commentary on shipping demand serve as a broad-based coincident indicator of US and global economic activity. As a leading indicator, UPS\'s quarterly results and forward guidance provide early signals of shifts in business-to-business shipping demand, e-commerce fulfillment volumes, inventory restocking cycles, and cross-border trade flows, dynamics that closely track industrial production, retail sales, and global trade conditions ahead of many official data releases. Quoted in US dollars per share and updated daily with closing prices.")
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r UPS, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "ups")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

<!-- ### Paypal -->

<!-- ```{r PAYPAL TXT, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} -->
<!-- series_info_box( -->
<!--   source_html = 'Yahoo — <a href="https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PYPL/" target="_blank">link</a>', -->
<!--   frequency = "Daily", -->
<!--   adjustment = "Not Adjusted", -->
<!--   about = "PayPal Holdings, Inc. (PYPL) is one of the world\'s largest digital payments platforms, operating a two-sided network connecting hundreds of millions of active consumer accounts and merchant accounts across more than 200 markets globally. Its core platform enables online and in-store payments, peer-to-peer money transfers, and buy-now-pay-later financing through its flagship PayPal wallet, the Venmo social payments app, the Braintree payment processing gateway, and the Xoom international remittance service. Because PayPal processes hundreds of billions of dollars in total payment volume annually across a broad range of consumer and business transactions, spanning e-commerce checkout, digital goods, travel, retail, and cross-border remittances, its payment volume trends, active account growth, and transactions per account are closely watched as a real-time barometer of digital consumer spending and the ongoing shift from cash and card payments to digital wallets. As a leading indicator, PayPal\'s quarterly results and forward guidance provide early signals of changes in online retail demand, consumer credit conditions through its credit and installment products, cross-border trade activity via its international corridors, and the pace of digital payment adoption, dynamics that often anticipate broader trends in personal consumption expenditures and e-commerce growth ahead of official data. Quoted in US dollars per share, updated in real time during market hours.") -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{=html} -->
<!-- <div class="perf-section"> -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{r PAYPAL, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE} -->

<!-- # Get series -->
<!-- data <- get_series(name = "paypal") -->

<!-- # Compute sma  -->
<!-- data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value") -->

<!-- # Plot serie -->
<!-- div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;", -->
<!--     produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)) -->

<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ```{=html} -->
<!-- </div> -->
<!-- ``` -->

<!-- ####################################################################### -->
<!-- Exchange Rates  -->
<!-- ####################################################################### -->

## Exchange Rates

```{=html}
<div class="company-hero">
  <div class="co-body">
    <div class="masthead-title">Exchange Rates</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

### EUR/USD

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r EURUSD, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "eur_usd")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### GBP/USD

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r GBPUSD, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "gbp_usd")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r CHFUSD, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}
# Get series

# Plot serie

  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r CADUSD, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "cad_usd")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```

### JPY/USD

```{=html}
<div class="perf-section">
```

```{r JPYUSD, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE}

# Get series
data <- get_series(name = "jpy_usd")

# Compute sma 
data <- compute_moving_average(df = data, date_col = "date", value_col = "value")

# Plot serie
tagList(
  div(style = "display: flex; justify-content: center;",
      produce_interactive_plot(df = data, sma = TRUE)),
  div(style = "text-align: center;",
      HTML("<small><em>Note : Simple Moving Averages are computed with the last 20, 50 and 200 closing prices</em></small>")))

```

```{=html}
</div>
```
